Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible. Current main still has the broad structured repair bridge plus positional plugin manifest, configured install, and registry repair contributions; the PR diff moves this bucket into structured checks and the PR body provides Crabbox after-fix CLI proof.

target.title: Doctor: continue structured repair conversion
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: after #83753, configured plugin install repair and plugin registry repair still had legacy positional doctor workflows, so they were not fully represented as structured health checks with consistent dry-run/diff proof.
- Solution: convert those repairs into structured doctor checks while preserving the existing positional flow and non-repair behavior.
- What changed: added reusable plugin registry issue detection, threaded release-source version metadata into structured repair context, added structured dry-run effects/diff entries, and covered the new paths with focused tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not add new CLI flags, change plugin install policy, or expose the internal `doctor --fix --dry-run --diff` path beyond the existing doctor repair flow.

This PR is intentionally stacked on #83753. The diff against `main` includes the #83753 commits; the new stage starts at `7216618010 fix(doctor): structure plugin registry repairs`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #83753
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

- Continue the structured doctor conversion started in #83753 for the next config/update/doctor/migrate bucket. These two repairs were still split between structured health reporting and positional legacy repair execution, which made dry-run/diff behavior and release-marker validation harder to prove consistently.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Structured doctor repair conversion for configured plugin install and plugin registry flows; lint no longer reports configured installs once the release repair marker is current; real CLI lint reports the intended structured checks.
- Real environment tested: Direct AWS Crabbox lease `cbx_95f6e9c97891` (`silver-crab`), OpenClaw source checkout synced from this branch, Node v24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile`; `pnpm build`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts --reporter=dot`; `node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json`; `node openclaw.mjs doctor --lint --only core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs --json` against old/current config metadata; `node openclaw.mjs doctor --lint --only core/doctor/plugin-registry --json` against an empty registry state.
- Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_40f3018e5b28` exited 0. Proof images are posted in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/84290#issuecomment-4491556493 and stored in https://gist.github.com/giodl73-repo/e41418e607535f7d29451ff7329e8e00. Local proof artifacts from that run: `.crabbox/proof/doctor-structured-proof-terminal.png`, `.crabbox/proof/doctor-structured-proof-terminal.mp4`, `.crabbox/proof/configured-old.json`, `.crabbox/proof/configured-current.json`, `.crabbox/proof/plugin-registry.json`, `.crabbox/logs/doctor-structured-proof-rerun.stdout.log`.
- Observed result after fix: build passed; focused tests passed 59 tests; release configured plugin installs tests passed 17 tests; tsgo passed; old configured install config reports `core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs`; current release marker returns `ok:true` with no findings; empty registry reports `core/doctor/plugin-registry`.
- What was not tested: full changed gate in direct Crabbox because the raw Crabbox sync path excludes `.git`; package, Docker, and update-survivor lanes were not run for this narrow structured repair slice.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): prior behavior was the legacy positional workflow: these repairs were not available as first-class structured health checks with matching structured dry-run effects/diff coverage.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the first structured doctor conversion left these two repairs behind as legacy positional checks, so structured reporting/repair metadata and dry-run diff evidence were not aligned with the repair execution path.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no focused tests asserted configured plugin release-marker lint behavior after repair, plugin registry lint detection without mutation, or structured dry-run effects for these two repair families.
- Contributing context (if known): #83753 established the conversion pattern; this PR applies it to the next bucket.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts`, `src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts`, `src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts`, `src/commands/doctor/shared/release-configured-plugin-installs.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: structured lint/repair/dry-run behavior for configured plugin install metadata and plugin registry state.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these are config/doctor seam behaviors; focused doctor tests cover the structured check contract without requiring package or live plugin install lanes.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): `src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts` and conversion-plan tests cover the broader repair orchestration.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Doctor lint/fix can now report and dry-run these two repair families through the structured health-check path while preserving existing repair order and behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
doctor repair flow -> positional configured-plugin/install-registry checks -> limited structured dry-run proof

After:
doctor repair flow -> structured health checks -> positional order preserved -> repair/dry-run/diff proof from one contract
```

## Dry-run Diff / Effects Catalog

This PR keeps dry-run reporting concrete: file/config changes get before/after diffs; package/state work gets explicit effect records instead of synthetic file diffs.

- `core/doctor/legacy-plugin-manifests`
  - Concrete diff: `kind: "file"`, `path: <plugin manifest>`, `before`: current manifest JSON, `after`: normalized manifest JSON with legacy capability metadata moved under `contracts`.
  - Effect: `kind: "file"`, action `would-rewrite-legacy-plugin-manifest-contracts` in dry-run and `rewrite-legacy-plugin-manifest-contracts` when applied, target `<plugin manifest>`, `dryRunSafe: true`.

- `core/doctor/configured-plugin-installs`
  - Concrete diff: `kind: "config"`, `path: "meta"`, `before`: current `cfg.meta`, `after`: config stamp containing the touched OpenClaw version and timestamp.
  - Package effects: `kind: "package"`, actions `would-install-configured-plugin` and `would-install-configured-channel-plugin`, targets the configured plugin/channel ids, `dryRunSafe: false`.
  - Config effect: `kind: "config"`, action `would-stamp-configured-plugin-install-release`, target `meta.lastTouchedVersion`, `dryRunSafe: true`.

- `core/doctor/plugin-registry`
  - State effect: `kind: "state"`, action `would-refresh-plugin-registry`, target the managed plugin registry, `dryRunSafe: true`.
  - Package effects: `kind: "package"`, actions `would-remove-stale-managed-npm-plugin` and `would-repair-openclaw-peer-link`, targets the affected managed package/plugin, `dryRunSafe: false`.
  - No fabricated diff is emitted for package-manager or registry-refresh work when doctor cannot represent it as a concrete local file/config before/after pair.

## Repair Validation / Detect-After

Invariant for all real structured repairs in this bucket: a successful non-dry-run repair is not considered fixed from its effect record alone. The shared repair runner immediately re-runs the same health check, scoped to the original finding paths, and records remaining findings if the install, cleanup, rewrite, or state update is still detectable. Dry-run remains preview-only because no mutation is applied.

- Legacy plugin manifest repairs rewrite the manifest, then `core/doctor/legacy-plugin-manifests` detection must no longer see the legacy top-level capability keys.
- Configured plugin install repair stamps `meta.lastTouchedVersion`; the scoped detect-after path must verify the release repair no longer needs to run for that repaired metadata.
- Plugin registry/package repairs refresh persisted registry/package state, then `core/doctor/plugin-registry` detection must no longer report stale registry, stale managed package, or broken peer-link issues. Lower-level tests also assert the package directory, package-lock, registry record, and peer-link state that make the detect-after pass meaningful.
- Skipped/disabled/operator-blocked paths are not counted as fixed and are intentionally not validated as repaired.

## Stack Refresh / Detect-After Test Placement

- Branch restacked and pushed with plugin-owned detect-after proof in this PR: `ff8e125577 test(doctor): validate effect-only repairs` and `2f9504cf77 test(doctor): validate plugin repair detection`.
- Bucket covered here: `core/doctor/legacy-plugin-manifests`, `core/doctor/plugin-registry`, and the shared effect-only repair-runner invariant.
- Exact focused proof after restack: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed 2 files / 20 tests; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts` passed.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL local checkout plus direct AWS Crabbox Linux VM proof
- Runtime/container: Node v24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0 in Crabbox
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): doctor CLI/config/plugin registry
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic OpenClaw config/registry states for old configured plugin metadata, current release metadata, and empty plugin registry

### Steps

1. Run focused doctor tests for plugin registry, core checks, health contributions, conversion plan, and repair flow.
2. Run release configured plugin install tests.
3. Run build, tsgo, and real doctor CLI lint against synthetic config/registry states in Crabbox.

### Expected

- Structured checks report stale configured-plugin and plugin-registry states when present and stay clean when release metadata is current.

### Actual

- Expected behavior observed in local tests and Crabbox run `run_40f3018e5b28`.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: local format, focused tests, release configured-plugin tests, tsgo, diff check, repeated autoreview, and Crabbox build/test/typecheck/CLI proof.
- Edge cases checked: current release marker does not produce a stale configured-plugin lint finding; old release marker does; empty plugin registry state reports the structured registry check; dry-run effects include config stamp behavior.
- What you did **not** verify: full changed gate, package/Docker/update survivor lanes.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: structured conversion could drift from the legacy positional repair behavior.
  - Mitigation: preserves positional contribution ordering and adds focused tests for repair context, dry-run effects, lint behavior, and post-repair validation.






## Stack Refresh: Note-Phase Contract

Restacked on #83753 after the detect/repair note contract landed.

- The plugin-registry converted health check no longer delegates its real repair path to the legacy note-printing helper.
- Added `repairPluginRegistryState()` as the structured repair path: it returns `changes` and `warnings` for registry rebuild/refresh, stale managed npm package cleanup, peer-link repair, disabled migration, and partial peer-link repair warnings without calling `note(...)`.
- The legacy `maybeRepairPluginRegistryState()` path remains available for old top-level doctor behavior until that path is converted.
- Detect remains structured findings-only for plugin registry issues; repair output now fits the #83753 phase model.

Additional verification for this refresh:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot
node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed result: 4 focused files / 37 tests passed, full repo `tsgo --noEmit` passed, formatting passed, whitespace check passed.


## Stack Refresh: Base Repair-Note Collector

Rebased onto refreshed #83753 so this plugin-registry repair branch inherits the finalized detect/repair note-phase contract. The base now injects and records `ctx.doctor.repairNote(...)` during `runDoctorHealthRepairs()`, so upper-stack structured repairs can preserve timing-sensitive operator presentation without importing terminal `note(...)`.

Additional verification after restack:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot
```

Observed result: 4 focused files / 37 tests passed on head `5ea4decea102a96f8a4327e20391a5277484c89c`.

target.title: Filter heartbeat response-tool transcript artifacts
target.body:
## Summary
- Filters full silent heartbeat transcript spans before normal prompt assembly, including helper tool calls/results, prompt-only interrupted wakes, pre-terminal assistant status text, and `heartbeat_respond notify:false`.
- Preserves visible heartbeat output only when it is actually visible: free-text alerts remain, and `heartbeat_respond notify:true` is kept only after a completed successful matching response-tool result.
- Fixes bundled LINE extension lint by using the public command definition type instead of deriving it through a broad API parameter.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Later normal prompts no longer receive stale silent heartbeat artifacts such as `[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]`, `HEARTBEAT.md`, `heartbeat_respond`, or `notify=false`; completed visible heartbeat notifications remain available to the next prompt context.
Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch at `20b0b80da792e128bb63d26d4bdcbba606e174c7`, using the production `runHeartbeatOnce` response-tool path and embedded runner context-engine normal-prompt path with test doubles for provider/channel delivery.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose`
Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal transcript from the focused run:
```text
✓ src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts > runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > treats notify=false as a quiet heartbeat ack
✓ src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts > runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > delivers notificationText when notify=true
✓ src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts > runHeartbeatOnce heartbeat response tool > uses the heartbeat response tool prompt in message-tool mode
✓ src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts > filters heartbeat response-tool transcript artifacts before normal prompt snapshots
✓ src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts > preserves visible heartbeat response-tool notifications in normal prompt snapshots
Test Files 3 passed (3)
Tests 100 passed (100)
```
Observed result after fix: The response-tool heartbeat runner treats `notify:false` as silent and delivers `notify:true` text; the following normal-prompt snapshot path strips `HEARTBEAT.md`, `heartbeat_respond`, `notify=false`, `"notify":false`, and the heartbeat prompt from both assembled context and `messagesSnapshot` while preserving completed visible `notify:true` heartbeat notification context.
What was not tested: No live external provider dispatch or Telegram delivery; the proof exercises OpenClaw's local runner, session-history, context assembly, and delivery-decision paths without network credentials.

## Verification
- `node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.browser-import.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.tool-response.test.ts --run --reporter=verbose` - `Test Files 7 passed (7)`, `Tests 154 passed (154)`.
- `pnpm lint --threads=8`
- `pnpm tsgo:prod`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `node scripts/run-bundled-extension-oxlint.mjs`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:compile`
- `pnpm run test:extensions:package-boundary:canary`
- `pnpm format:check -- extensions/line/index.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.ts src/auto-reply/heartbeat-filter.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-injection.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --uncommitted > /tmp/openclaw-heartbeat-codex-review-final2.log 2>&1` - no actionable findings.

target.title: fix(voice-call): persist assistant transcript on call.speaking events
target.body:
## Summary

`onTranscript(role, text, isFinal)` in the realtime bridge fires `call.speaking` events with the assistant's finalized utterance in `event.text`, but the manager's `call.speaking` handler only transitions state and drops the text. That means realtime calls (Google Gemini Live, etc.) finish with `call.transcript` containing only the user side; the bot side is never written. The Telnyx classic path (`call.speak.started` → `call.speaking` with text) has the same gap. After the call ends, the only record of what Rosellen said is the SSE event stream, which isn't persisted.

This change has the `call.speaking` handler in `extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.ts` push a `"bot"` transcript entry when `event.text` is a non-empty string. State transition is unchanged; empty/whitespace text is ignored (deltas and TTS no-text marks).

`TranscriptEntrySchema.speaker` is already `"bot" | "user"`, so no schema change. Two unit tests added to `events.test.ts` covering the populated and empty-text branches.

## Verification

- **Behavior addressed**: After a realtime (e.g. Gemini Live) inbound call ends, `call.transcript` contained only `speaker:"user"` entries; everything Rosellen said was missing. Same gap was visible on Telnyx classic TTS calls.
- **Real environment tested**: Vitest unit, plus end-to-end against a live Telnyx → Tailscale Funnel → openclaw gateway path with Google Gemini Live realtime on a local Strix Halo box.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/voice-call/src/manager/events.test.ts`
  - Patched the equivalent line in the installed bundle `runtime-entry-*.js` on the live host, restarted `openclaw-gateway.service`, placed an inbound test call.
- **Evidence after fix**: Local vitest run reports `Test Files  1 passed (1) | Tests  17 passed (17)`. The two new test cases (`persists assistant transcript when call.speaking events carry text`, `does not push empty-text call.speaking events to the transcript`) pass.
- **Observed result after fix**: New rows in `~/.openclaw/voice-calls/calls.jsonl` for completed realtime calls now include both `speaker:"user"` and `speaker:"bot"` entries in `transcript`.
- **What was not tested**: Twilio Media Streams realtime path (don't have Twilio infra locally); the full Crabbox/E2E lane; any non-realtime non-Telnyx provider. Behavior is restricted to the `call.speaking` handler and gated on a non-empty `event.text`, so providers that don't pass text continue to behave exactly as before.

target.title: feat(discord): archive ACP-created threads on /acp close
target.body:
## Summary
When `/acp close` is used inside a Discord thread that OpenClaw itself created for an ACP session, OpenClaw now:
1. closes the ACP session
2. unbinds the thread/session
3. archives the Discord thread

This reduces stale task-thread clutter while preserving history.

## Scope
In scope:
- Discord threads only
- ACP-created threads only
- archive, not delete

Out of scope:
- Telegram topics / Slack reply threads / generic cross-channel conversation closing
- deleting threads
- archiving manually attached or reused existing threads
- idle-time auto-archive

## Behavior
- ACP-created Discord thread: `/acp close` closes, unbinds, then archives the thread
- manually attached/reused thread: existing close behavior remains unchanged
- archive failure: close/unbind still succeed and the reply is downgraded to a partial-success warning
- missing ownership metadata fails safe (no archive)

## Implementation
- Thread binding metadata now records whether the thread was spawned by ACP
- `/acp close` checks that metadata before requesting Discord thread archive
- Discord outbound payload handling supports archive-after-reply so the success message is sent before the thread is archived

## Tests
Added/updated tests for:
- ACP-created Discord thread bindings storing spawned metadata
- `/acp close` archiving ACP-created Discord threads
- `/acp close` not archiving manually attached Discord threads
- fail-safe behavior when ownership metadata is incomplete
- non-Discord behavior unchanged
- Discord outbound archive-after-reply success and partial-failure behavior

Executed:
- `npx vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp.test.ts src/channels/plugins/outbound/discord.test.ts src/discord/monitor/thread-bindings.lifecycle.test.ts`

target.title: Add configurable outputDir for session-memory hook
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: `session-memory` always writes `/new` and `/reset` snapshots into `<workspace>/memory/`, which mixes session continuity artifacts into active semantic memory.
- Why it matters: agents using `memory_search` or local embeddings end up indexing session dumps alongside long-term notes, which adds noise and indexing cost.
- What changed: added an optional `hooks.internal.entries.session-memory.outputDir` config, kept the default as `memory`, validated it as workspace-relative, and documented the new behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): default output location, slug generation, transcript extraction, and the hook's enabled/disabled semantics.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `session-memory` now accepts optional `outputDir`, relative to the agent workspace.
- Defaults remain unchanged: if `outputDir` is omitted or invalid, files still land in `<workspace>/memory/`.
- Docs now consistently describe both `/new` and `/reset` behavior.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS (Apple Silicon)
- Runtime/container: local Node.js + pnpm workspace
- Model/provider: N/A for verification; slug generator is mocked in unit tests
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A

target.title: Feature(memory-lancedb): Agent Scoping #38797
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The `memory-lancedb` plugin shared a single global memory pool across all agents. An agent could recall memories captured by a different agent, and explicit tool calls (`memory_recall`, `memory_store`, `memory_forget`) had no way to scope reads/writes to the calling agent.
- **Why it matters:** In multi-agent setups this causes cross-contamination: agent A sees agent B's memories, and agent B can overwrite or forget agent A's entries via tool calls
- **What changed:** Added an opt-in `agentScoping` config boolean. When enabled, memories are tagged with the originating `agentId` at capture time; recall and tool calls filter to that agent's memories plus legacy globally-shared memories (`agentId = ""`). A best-effort migration adds the `agentId` column to existing LanceDB tables. Tool registration switched from static objects to factories so each agent-run gets its own scoped context.
- **What did NOT change:** Default behavior (`agentScoping === false`) is fully backward-compatible — the global memory pool is preserved and no existing data is altered.

## Change Type

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #38797

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New config option `agentScoping` (boolean, default `false`) in the `memory-lancedb` plugin settings.
- When `agentScoping === true`:
  - Auto-captured memories are tagged with the originating agent's ID.
  - Auto-recall and all memory tools (`memory_recall`, `memory_store`, `memory_forget`) only see memories belonging to the current agent plus legacy untagged (globally shared) memories.
- When `agentScoping === false` (default): no behavior change.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? `es
  - **Risk:** When `agentScoping === true`, agents cannot read each other's scoped memories. The `agentId` value passed through `ctx.agentId` is used in a SQL filter string. A malformed `agentId` could be used for SQL injection against the LanceDB filter.
  - **Mitigation:** `agentId` is validated against `/^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-.:@]{0,128}$/` before interpolation; values that do not match are rejected, not silently stripped.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux (WSL2)
- Runtime/container: Node 22 / Bun
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: memory-lancedb extension
- Relevant config: `agentScoping === true` in plugin config

### Steps

1. Enable `agentScoping === true` in the `memory-lancedb` plugin config.
2. Run two agents with different `agentId` values and store a memory from each.
3. Recall from each agent. Each agent should only see its own memories and untagged legacy entries.

### Expected

- Agent A's recall returns only Agent A's memories and global memories.
- Agent B's recall returns only Agent B's memories and global memories.

### Actual

- Before this change: both agents see all memories.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

```
BEFORE:
FAIL extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts > memory plugin e2e > passes configured dimensions to OpenAI embeddings API
TypeError: recallTool.execute is not a function

AFTER:
✓ extensions/memory-lancedb/index.test.ts > memory plugin e2e (12 passed, 1 skipped)
```

## Human Verification

- Verified scenarios: all unit tests pass; `agentScoping: false` path exercises existing code unchanged; migration skips gracefully when `agentId` column already present.
- Edge cases checked: legacy tables without `agentId` column get it added with `''` default on first open; migration failure is caught and does not crash the plugin.
- What you did **not** verify: live multi-agent end-to-end with a real LanceDB instance and real agent IDs.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — `agentScoping` defaults to `false`; existing behavior unchanged.
- Config/env changes? Yes — new optional `agentScoping` boolean in plugin config.
- Migration needed? No — existing LanceDB tables are migrated automatically on first open; existing memories are preserved as globally-shared by setting `agentId` empty string.

## Failure Recovery

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: set `agentScoping` to false in plugin config.
- Files/config to restore: `extensions/memory-lancedb/config.ts`, `extensions/memory-lancedb/index.ts`.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: memories not appearing in recall after enabling `agentScoping` (likely the `agentId` is not being propagated through `ctx` in that hook context).

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: `agentId` injection via `ctx.agentId` into the LanceDB SQL filter string.
  - Mitigation: strict regex allowlist (`/^[a-zA-Z0-9_\-.:@]{0,128}$/`) rejects unexpected values before interpolation.
- Risk: Migration (`addColumns`) fails silently on unusual LanceDB versions, leaving the table without the `agentId` column. Scoped filtering is then skipped.
  - Mitigation: best-effort catch with logger warning; plugin continues to function in unscoped mode.

target.title: Doctor: convert interactive maintenance checks
target.body:
## Contract Split

This PR is the first real doctor repair switchover after the structured health-check contract landed. The contract split is:

```ts
detect(ctx, scope?) -> HealthFinding[]
repair?(ctx, findings) -> HealthRepairResult
```

`detect()` is the read-only source of truth for `doctor --lint` and post-repair validation. `repair()` is the mutating path used by `doctor --fix`. The migration goal is isomorphism: every converted repair should preserve the old user-facing behavior and contribution order while gaining a provable `detect -> repair -> detect` loop.

This slice converts the first real maintenance repairs:

- shell completion detection and cached-completion repair
- startup channel maintenance repair at the startup-channel contribution slot
- Linux systemd linger detection and guided repair for local Gateway services
- browser profile residue repair kept at the browser contribution slot from the prior repair slice

The important shape is that converted repairs no longer run from one early bulk `doctor:structured-health-repairs` pass. Each converted contribution invokes its mapped health check from its existing doctor position, so the contract changes without changing repair sequencing.

## Dry-Run, Diff, And Isomorphism

This PR also threads the future repair-preview contract through the converted paths:

- `dryRun` means the repair path can report what it would do without applying it.
- `diff: true` asks repair code to include structured config/file diffs when the repair has a renderable patch.
- `diff: false` does not skip the repair; it just means no structured diff payload is requested.
- side-effect repairs such as shell setup or systemd linger report `effects[]` instead of pretending to have a config/file diff.

The public doctor dry-run/diff mode should still be enabled at the end of the migration, not here. This branch prepares the contract and proves these first repairs behave isomorphically. Future repair branches should either return `diffs[]`, return side-effect `effects[]`, or explicitly document why preview is not meaningful for that repair.

## Migration Plan

This is an active staged migration, not a one-off refactor:

1. Land the contract and read-only checks. Done by the earlier doctor health-check PRs.
2. Convert real repairs in small buckets while preserving contribution order. This PR is the interactive-maintenance bucket.
3. For every converted repair, prove `detect -> repair -> detect` with focused tests and, where feasible, real CLI proof from disposable state.
4. Backfill dry-run/diff/effects metadata for each repair as it moves.
5. After the repair inventory is converted or classified, expose the user-facing doctor dry-run/diff mode with honest output for every repair category.

The remaining migration is currently planned as 6 buckets:

- legacy plugin manifests and configured plugin registry/install repairs
- sandbox registry, sandbox image, and sandbox scope repairs
- auth-profile repairs, including flat stores, OAuth sidecars, OAuth ids, keychain readiness, and Codex provider overrides
- session/state cleanup, including state integrity, Codex session routes, session locks, session transcripts, config-audit scrub, and legacy cron storage
- gateway/service repairs, including Gateway service config, Gateway daemon drift, memory recall/search repair, and prepared runtime-fact consumers
- final dry-run/diff enablement, once every repair bucket has either `diffs[]`, side-effect `effects[]`, or an explicit non-previewable classification with proof
## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Converted doctor maintenance repairs now run through structured health checks at their original doctor contribution positions, while preserving user-facing repair behavior and adding scoped post-repair validation.

Real environment tested: Current PR head `8cef0bb2833a683b1f681aa72defc10812c852bb` was tested in two ways: focused Windows source-checkout tests, plus a disposable Azure Ubuntu 24.04 VM with a real user systemd manager and real `loginctl` linger state.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Azure proof ran current-head `doctor --lint` and `doctor --fix --yes` against disposable shell-completion and systemd-linger states. Focused proof ran:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/commands/systemd-linger.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=verbose
node node_modules\oxfmt\bin\oxfmt --check --threads=1 src\commands\doctor-completion.test.ts src\commands\doctor.types.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.ts src\flows\doctor-core-checks.test.ts src\flows\doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.ts src\flows\doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src\flows\doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src\flows\doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts
node node_modules\@typescript\native-preview\bin\tsgo.js -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false
```

Evidence after fix:

- Azure CLI transcript: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/azure-doctor-cli-proof.redacted.log
- Azure proof excerpt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/azure-proof-excerpt.txt
- Azure proof summary: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/summary.redacted.txt
- Focused test transcript: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof.clean.log
- Focused proof excerpt: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/proof-excerpt.txt
- Artifact README: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/real-proof-8cef0bb/README.md
- Docker repro for the non-privileged shell-completion path: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/giodl73-repo/openclaw/proof-artifacts/pr-83753-isomorphism/pr-83753/azure-cli-proof-8cef0bb/docker-repro.sh

Observed result after fix:

```text
shell completion lint before fix exit=1 expected=1
shell completion doctor fix exit=0 expected=0
shell completion lint after fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd user manager status exit=0 expected=0
systemd disposable unit enabled exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger before fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint before fix exit=1 expected=1
systemd linger doctor fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger after fix exit=0 expected=0
systemd linger lint after fix exit=0 expected=0
```

The focused proof also shows 7 focused files and 58 tests passing, changed-file formatting passing, and the core test TypeScript config exiting 0.

What was not tested: Docker was used only for the non-privileged shell-completion repro. The privileged systemd/loginctl behavior was intentionally proven on a disposable Azure VM instead of Docker because containerized loginctl proof is less representative than a real Linux user manager.

## Verification

- Focused Vitest proof: 7 files / 58 tests passed.
- Changed-file formatting: passed.
- Core test `tsgo`: passed.
- Disposable Azure CLI proof: shell-completion and systemd-linger both showed before-detect, repair, and after-detect success on current head.




## Stack Refresh: Repair Presentation Notes

This branch now also establishes the shared presentation-note contract for the rest of the doctor repair stack.

- `HealthRepairResult` can return ordered `notes[]` as `{ message, title }` presentation data.
- `runDoctorHealthRepairs()` preserves those notes alongside `changes`, `warnings`, `diffs`, and `effects`.
- The doctor contribution layer prints returned notes immediately after the structured repair call and before generic `Doctor changes` / `Doctor warnings` summaries, preserving contribution order without letting low-level helpers print directly.
- Note suppression still stays centralized in `note(...)`, so `OPENCLAW_SUPPRESS_NOTES` continues to suppress presentation output at the doctor layer.
- Existing repair callers forward notes from helper results where the helper contract already has them, so follow-on branches can remove direct `note(...)` calls from repair helpers without changing the runner again.

Additional verification for this refresh:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=dot
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/health-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed result: 2 focused files / 36 tests passed, formatting passed, whitespace check passed.


## Stack Refresh: Detect/Repair Note Contract

This supersedes the earlier repair-only note wording in this PR body. The base contract now separates presentation by phase:

- `detect()` may return the legacy-compatible `HealthFinding[]` or the new `{ findings, notes }` object.
- Detect notes are read-only presentation context for normal `openclaw doctor`; they stay separate from lintable findings.
- `doctor --lint` normalizes detect output but keeps `notes` separate from `findings`, so lint exit codes and JSON-facing findings are not polluted by informational doctor notes.
- `runDoctorHealthRepairs()` now records `detectionNotes` separately from `repairNotes`.
- Repair implementations can return final `notes`, and can also use `ctx.doctor.repairNote(...)` for timing-sensitive repair presentation that must appear before/between repair steps without importing terminal UI.
- The doctor contribution layer owns printing, so `OPENCLAW_SUPPRESS_NOTES` remains centralized in `note(...)` and low-level helpers do not need terminal side effects.

Additional verification for this refinement:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot
node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/health-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed result: 6 focused files / 55 tests passed, full repo `tsgo --noEmit` passed, formatting passed, whitespace check passed.


## Stack Refresh: Converted Rules Use Note Phases

The converted checks in this PR now use the detect/repair presentation contract directly:

- Note-capturing readiness checks such as Claude CLI and browser readiness preserve warning/error-like output as findings, while informational note output is returned as detect notes instead of being dropped or printed from the helper.
- Workspace backup and memory-system suggestions are detect notes rather than lint findings, so normal `openclaw doctor` can render them without making `doctor --lint` noisy.
- The systemd linger repair emits its pre-confirmation/operator context through `ctx.doctor.repairNote(...)`, preserving repair-time ordering while keeping terminal printing owned by the doctor layer.

Additional verification for this refresh:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts --reporter=dot
node node_modules/@typescript/native-preview/bin/tsgo.js --noEmit --pretty false -p tsconfig.json
pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/health-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-browser-residue-check.test.ts src/flows/doctor-startup-channel-maintenance.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Observed result: 6 focused files / 55 tests passed, full repo `tsgo --noEmit` passed, formatting passed, whitespace check passed.


## Stack Refresh: Repair-Note Sink Collector

The repair-note sink is now fully wired in the base #83753 contract. `runDoctorHealthRepairs()` injects a per-check `ctx.doctor.repairNote(...)` collector when invoking `repair()`, records those timing-sensitive notes in `repairNotes`, and forwards them to any outer sink. This makes the contract usable for upper-stack repairs such as sandbox and gateway helpers that need to present operator context before or between repair steps without calling `note(...)` directly.

Additional verification for this refresh:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts --reporter=dot
```

Observed result: 3 focused files / 20 tests passed on head `2417269a00362a2de527962a00364100927137c2`.

target.title: fix(cron): filter reasoning/thinking payloads from announce delivery
target.body:
## Summary

- Filters reasoning/thinking payloads from cron announce delivery pipeline end-to-end
- Propagates `isReasoning` flag through `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` `.map()` return and `EmbeddedPiRunResult` type
- Filters reasoning in all 3 payload-selection helpers + `firstText` fallback + error-recovery detection
- Reasoning payloads are **never** deliverable and **never** mask error status

### Root cause

The `isReasoning` flag was correctly set internally by `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads()` but:
1. Not propagated in the `.map()` return value (`payloads.ts`)
2. Not declared on the `EmbeddedPiRunResult` payload type (`types.ts`)
3. Not filtered in cron delivery helpers (`helpers.ts`)
4. Not filtered in `firstText` fallback (`run.ts:754`)
5. Not excluded from error-recovery detection (`run.ts:844`)

### Commits
1. `39d426d` — Filter reasoning in helpers + add `isReasoning` to `EmbeddedPiRunResult` type
2. `3877d9a` — Fix `firstText` fallback bypass (Codex review)
3. `b003338` — Propagate `isReasoning` in `buildEmbeddedRunPayloads` `.map()` (Greptile review)
4. `402ba05` — Exclude reasoning from `hasSuccessfulPayloadAfterLastError` (Codex P2 review)

### Reasoning-only scenario trace (run.ts:754-764)

| Variable | Value | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `firstText` | `""` | `.find((p) => !p.isReasoning)` finds nothing |
| `pickSummaryFromPayloads()` | `undefined` | Both passes skip `isReasoning` |
| `pickLastNonEmptyTextFromPayloads()` | `undefined` | Both passes skip `isReasoning` |
| `synthesizedText` | `undefined` | Both sources undefined |
| `pickLastDeliverablePayload()` | `undefined` | Both passes skip `isReasoning` |
| `hasSuccessfulPayloadAfterLastError` | `false` | Reasoning excluded from recovery check |
| **`deliveryPayloads`** | **`[]`** | Nothing delivered |

Closes #40480

target.title: exec-approvals: pre-fill /approve commands in chat notifications
target.body:
## Problem

The current exec approval notification shows a generic usage hint:

```
Reply with: /approve <id> allow-once|allow-always|deny
```

This forces the user to manually compose the command: copy the UUID from the `ID:` line above, type `/approve`, paste the ID, then type the decision. On mobile (Telegram, etc.) this is especially painful — switching between fields, selecting the UUID without grabbing extra characters, and typing the decision by hand. A single approval can take 15-20 seconds of fiddly text editing instead of one tap.

## Solution

Replace the generic hint with three ready-to-use commands wrapped in inline code:

```
`/approve daf7dad0-7a24-45bc-a68b-c46d0a1707c1 allow-once`
`/approve daf7dad0-7a24-45bc-a68b-c46d0a1707c1 allow-always`
`/approve daf7dad0-7a24-45bc-a68b-c46d0a1707c1 deny`
```

In Telegram (and most chat clients), tapping inline code copies the full string to clipboard. The user then pastes and sends — three taps instead of manual text assembly.

### Future improvement

One-tap approve via Telegram inline keyboard buttons (`reply_markup.inline_keyboard`) would provide an even better UX and could be explored as a follow-up.

## Test plan

- [x] Run a command outside the exec allowlist to trigger an approval notification
- [x] Verify the notification contains three separate inline-code lines with the full approval ID
- [x] Verify tapping a line in Telegram copies the complete command
- [x] Verify all three decisions (`allow-once`, `allow-always`, `deny`) resolve correctly
- [x] Verify the agent's own response does not duplicate the approve commands

Closes #40477

target.title: fix(tui): refresh on external session reset and clear stale run state
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: TUI did not refresh when the current session was reset externally (for example via `/new` or `/reset` from another client), so it could keep showing stale transcript/token state.
- Why it matters: users could see outdated conversation state in TUI even though the gateway session had already been reset, and stale tool/run state could still interfere with the refreshed view.
- What changed: `sessions.reset` now broadcasts a lightweight `session` reset event; TUI now listens for that event, refreshes history/session info for the current session, and clears stale in-memory run tracking state.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this PR does not redesign the broader event model, does not change session storage format, and does not fully solve all theoretically late stale `chat` events that are still keyed only by `sessionKey`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #38966
- Related None

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

List user-visible changes (including defaults/config).  
If none, write `None`.

- TUI now refreshes automatically when the current session is reset externally.
- After an external reset, stale tool/run state is cleared before the refreshed session view is shown.
- No config or default behavior changes for users outside this reset flow.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows (development), issue originally reported on macOS
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): TUI + external session reset flow
- Relevant config (redacted): default local test setup

### Steps

1. Open TUI on a shared/current session.
2. Reset the same session externally (for example via another client using `/new` or `/reset`).
3. Observe TUI behavior before and after this patch.

### Expected

- TUI should refresh history/session info for the current session after the external reset.
- Old run/tool state should not continue to affect the refreshed view.

### Actual

- Before this fix, TUI did not automatically refresh on external reset.
- Stale run/tool state could remain in memory and affect subsequent handling.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: reviewed the reset flow end-to-end in code; verified that `sessions.reset` now broadcasts a `session` reset event; verified that TUI handles that event by reloading history/session info and clearing stale run state.
- Edge cases checked: reset event for a different session is ignored; stale tool events from an old run are ignored after external reset.
- What you did **not** verify: I did not manually reproduce the full multi-client runtime flow on macOS; verification here is based on targeted TUI handler tests and code-path review.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert this PR or remove the new `session` event broadcast/handling changes.
- Files/config to restore:
  - `src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts`
  - `src/tui/tui.ts`
  - `src/tui/tui-types.ts`
  - `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.ts`
  - `src/tui/tui-event-handlers.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - TUI not refreshing on external reset
  - unexpected extra refreshes for unrelated sessions
  - stale tool/run events still appearing after reset

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: a `session` reset event could trigger an unnecessary refresh for the wrong session if session matching is incorrect.
  - Mitigation: TUI only handles the event when `sessionKey` matches the current session.
- Risk: stale in-memory run state could continue affecting post-reset behavior.
  - Mitigation: clear `activeChatRunId`, `sessionRuns`, `finalizedRuns`, local run IDs, and recreate `streamAssembler` on current-session reset.
- Risk: late stale `chat` events are still not perfectly distinguishable if they share the same `sessionKey`.
  - Mitigation: scope this PR to the core reset-refresh bug and explicitly leave stricter late-chat disambiguation as follow-up work.

target.title: feat(runtime): expose agent and session env markers
target.body:
## Summary

This PR exposes two deterministic runtime markers to spawned child processes across several OpenClaw shell/runtime entry points:

- `OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID`
- `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY` (when a session key is available)

This now covers:

- `exec`
- `acp-client`
- `tui-local`

Existing shell markers remain unchanged:

- `OPENCLAW_SHELL=exec`
- `OPENCLAW_SHELL=acp-client`
- `OPENCLAW_SHELL=tui-local`

## Motivation

In multi-agent deployments, downstream scripts often need to branch on **which agent is currently running**. Before this change there was no stable runtime marker for that, so users had to rely on brittle heuristics such as:

- inferring identity from prompt/persona text,
- guessing from workspace layout,
- or parsing internal session-key structure opportunistically.

That is awkward and unreliable for automation.

A concrete example from my setup: multiple cooperating agents share scripting paths for commit attribution, config selection, and side-effect routing. Those scripts need a deterministic identity contract, not a prompt-level guess.

This PR provides:

- `OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID` for stable identity
- `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY` for richer per-session context

## What changed

### `exec`
- Added `resolveExecRuntimeEnv(...)` in `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts`
- `runExecProcess(...)` now injects:
  - `OPENCLAW_SHELL=exec`
  - `OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID`
  - `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY` (when available)
- `OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID` prefers an explicit `agentId` and otherwise falls back to deriving from the session key
- Fixed an explicit `agentId` propagation gap in `createExecTool(...)` so legacy/global contexts do not incorrectly collapse to `main`

### `acp-client`
- `resolveAcpClientSpawnEnv(...)` now accepts optional agent/session context
- When provided, the spawned bridge process receives:
  - `OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID`
  - `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY`

### `tui-local`
- Added a local-shell env resolver for the TUI `!` shell path
- When the current TUI session is bound to an agent/session, the spawned local shell receives:
  - `OPENCLAW_AGENT_ID`
  - `OPENCLAW_SESSION_KEY`

## Tests

Added/updated tests covering:

- exec runtime env resolution helper behavior
- host=gateway exec env injection
- PTY exec env injection
- explicit agent-id preservation in legacy/global exec contexts
- ACP client env injection
- TUI local-shell env injection

Commands run locally:

```bash
corepack pnpm vitest run \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec.path.test.ts \
  src/agents/bash-tools.exec.pty.test.ts \
  src/acp/client.test.ts \
  src/tui/tui-local-shell.test.ts
```

## Documentation

Updated docs to clarify that runtime-injected identity/session markers are no longer exec-only and now apply (when context exists) to:

- `exec`
- `acp-client`
- `tui-local`

## Notes

I intentionally did **not** widen this to the ACP backend runtime marker (`OPENCLAW_SHELL=acp`) in the same PR. That path lives across the `acpx` extension/runtime boundary and seemed better as a follow-up change after landing the lower-risk entry points above.

target.title: config: use datetime suffix for config backup files instead of numeric rotation
target.body:
Closes #39923

## Summary

Replaces the numeric rotation ring (`openclaw.json.bak.1`–`.bak.4`) with datetime-stamped backup files (`openclaw.json.bak.YYYYMMDD-HHmmssSSS`, UTC). This makes it immediately clear *when* each backup was taken without needing to inspect file metadata — directly useful when recovering from a bad config change.

**Before:**
```
openclaw.json.bak
openclaw.json.bak.1   ← newest rotation slot
openclaw.json.bak.2
openclaw.json.bak.3
openclaw.json.bak.4   ← oldest rotation slot
```

**After:**
```
openclaw.json.bak
openclaw.json.bak.20260308-143022456   ← most recent
openclaw.json.bak.20260308-130015000
openclaw.json.bak.20260308-110000000
openclaw.json.bak.20260308-090000000   ← oldest kept
```

## Changes

- `formatBackupTimestamp(date)` — new helper producing `YYYYMMDD-HHmmssSSS` (UTC, millisecond precision to avoid collision on rapid saves; lexicographically sortable without reading file metadata)
- `BACKUP_DATETIME_RE` — exported regex for matching valid datetime suffixes
- `rotateConfigBackups` — now renames `.bak` → `.bak.<timestamp>` instead of chaining renames across numbered slots; accepts optional `now` param for deterministic testing
- `hardenBackupPermissions` — scans directory for datetime-pattern `.bak.*` entries and chmods them (in addition to the primary `.bak`)
- `cleanOrphanBackups` — replaces the `validSuffixes` integer set with datetime-pattern matching; deletes all non-datetime `.bak.*` files (including legacy `.bak.1`–`.bak.4`); keeps only the `CONFIG_BACKUP_COUNT - 1` most recent datetime backups via lexicographic sort
- `maintainConfigBackups` — passes `now` through to `rotateConfigBackups`; `readdir` promoted to required on `BackupMaintenanceFs` (production `fs.promises` always has it)

## Test plan

- `formatBackupTimestamp` — UTC formatting, padding, lexicographic sort order, regex match
- Ring test — 6 writes with fixed timestamps confirms 4 datetime backups kept, oldest pruned, `.bak` holds most-recent-minus-one
- `hardenBackupPermissions` — verifies 0o600 on both `.bak` and `.bak.<datetime>` (POSIX only)
- `cleanOrphanBackups` — legacy numeric/PID/manual orphans deleted; datetime files preserved; limit enforcement with `CONFIG_BACKUP_COUNT + 1` overflow
- `maintainConfigBackups` — full compose flow: prior `.bak` gets a datetime stamp, new `.bak` created, permissions hardened, orphans pruned

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(agents): retry transient provider errors when single profile has no fallback
target.body:
## Summary

When a single auth profile encounters transient provider errors (overloaded, rate_limit, server_error) and no fallback model is configured, the agent stops immediately instead of retrying on the same profile.

This PR adds retry logic for transient provider errors when:
- Only one auth profile exists
- No fallback model is configured  
- The error is transient (overloaded, rate_limit, server_error)

Non-transient errors (auth failures) and cases where a fallback is configured continue to use existing failover behavior.

## Changes

- Add  in  to classify transient errors
- Add retry-on-same-profile path in  when single profile + transient error
- Add 6 new test cases covering: overloaded retry, rate_limit retry, server_error retry, auth NOT retried, fallback configured NOT retried, and isTransientFailoverReason unit tests
- Update CHANGELOG.md

Closes #84236

---

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Retry transient provider errors (overloaded, rate_limit, server_error) on the same auth profile when only one profile exists and no fallback model is configured, instead of stopping the agent immediately
- **Real environment tested**: Linux RHEL 8 (kernel 4.19.112), Node v22.22.0, worktree /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84236, HEAD 31d59196cb113c7f7699c53df0d36ea160bb9a0b, isolated HOME/OPENCLAW_HOME /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84236/.automation/openclaw-home, Port 19842
- **OpenClaw startup command**:
```bash
HOME=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84236/.automation/openclaw-home OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84236/.automation/openclaw-home OPENCLAW_PORT=19842 node /media/vdc/code/ai/openclaw-84236/openclaw.mjs gateway --allow-unconfigured
```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
1. pnpm build — completed successfully
2. pnpm test src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/failover-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.test.ts — 50 tests passed

target.title: fix(config): warn when configured model primary is not in catalog
target.body:
## Summary

When a user sets `agents.defaults.model.primary` or `agents.list[].model` to a model name that does not exist in the runtime catalog (e.g. `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-codex` before the Codex provider ships it), the config is accepted silently. Every cron job and heartbeat then fails at runtime with `FailoverError: Unknown model`, and the user has no indication why without reading raw error logs.

This adds a lightweight validation pass that checks configured model primaries against the loaded model catalog and surfaces warnings through `openclaw doctor`. Warnings, not errors, because forward-compat model names are intentional and the model may appear later.

## What changed

- `collectModelConfigWarnings(config, catalog)` in `src/config/validation.ts`: iterates `agents.defaults.model.primary` and each `agents.list[].model`, resolves them through the existing `resolveModelRefFromString` path, and returns `ConfigValidationIssue[]` when any ref is absent from the catalog.
- `src/commands/doctor.ts`: loads the model catalog once and calls `collectModelConfigWarnings`. Reuses the same catalog instance for the existing `hooks.gmail.model` check to avoid a redundant `loadModelCatalog` call.

## What did NOT change

No runtime behavior, no config schema, no model resolution logic, no failover paths. This is purely an advisory check surfaced through `doctor`.

## Linked Issues

- Closes #39811
- Related #39818
- Related #37623

## Validation

```
pnpm vitest run src/config/validation.model-warnings.test.ts  # 6/6 pass
pnpm build                                                     # clean
pnpm check                                                     # clean
```

## Tests

6 targeted tests in `src/config/validation.model-warnings.test.ts`:
- valid model in catalog produces no warning
- unknown default model primary triggers warning
- string-form model config triggers warning
- unknown per-agent model triggers warning
- no model configured produces no warning
- empty catalog still warns (cannot validate)

target.title: feat(web-search): expose Brave Goggles for custom search filtering and ranking
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Brave Search supports [Goggles](https://search.brave.com/help/goggles), a [query-time DSL](https://brave.com/static-assets/files/goggles.pdf) that redefines how results are ranked at the index level (not client-side post-filtering), but OpenClaw's `web_search` tool has no way to pass them.
- Why it matters: Agents can construct task-specific rules at query time to scope grounding content — e.g. restrict to academic sources, boost official docs over SEO tutorials, suppress low-signal domains — without burning API quota and context window on irrelevant results. The DSL supports site/URL pattern matching (`$site=`, `$inurl=`, wildcards) and ranking actions (`$boost=N`, `$downrank=N`, `$discard`). Rules can be passed inline (newline-separated) or referenced by hosted URL ([quickstart](https://github.com/brave/goggles-quickstart)). Goggles work on both Brave endpoints OpenClaw uses: web search and [llm-context](https://docs.openclaw.ai/brave-search).
- What changed:
  - Added `goggles` string parameter to the Brave tool schema (`src/agents/tools/web-search.ts`) with a self-contained description including DSL syntax and examples so the agent can use it without reading docs
  - Wired `goggles` through to both web and llm-context URL builders
  - Added Brave-only provider validation (`unsupported_goggles` error for non-Brave providers)
  - Included `goggles` in cache keys (different goggles = separate cache entries)
  - Updated `docs/tools/web.md` with paired Goggles + Perplexity examples for domain filtering
  - Updated `docs/brave-search.md` with parameter table entry and usage notes
- What did NOT change (scope boundary):
  - Per-query only — no config schema changes (goggles is not static config)
  - No news search support (OpenClaw doesn't use that endpoint)
  - No changes to other providers
  - No test infrastructure changes

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

None

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `web_search` tool gains an optional `goggles` string parameter (Brave provider only)
- Non-Brave providers return an `unsupported_goggles` error if `goggles` is passed
- Different goggles values produce separate cache entries

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No new endpoints — goggles is appended as a query parameter on existing Brave web search and llm-context API calls.
- Command/tool execution surface changed? Minimal — adds one optional string parameter to an existing tool; no new tool or command.
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any
- Runtime/container: Node 22+
- Model/provider: Brave Search API
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): Brave API key configured

### Steps

1. `pnpm build` — no TS errors
2. `pnpm test -- web-search.test` — all 40 tests pass
3. `pnpm check` — clean
4. Live API: inline goggles (`$discard\n$site=docs.rs\n$site=rust-lang.org`) returned only docs.rs and rust-lang.org results on both web search and llm-context endpoints

### Expected

- Build, tests, and format all pass
- Live queries with goggles return results scoped to the specified rules

### Actual

- All pass as expected

### Examples

```
// Restrict to trusted academic sources
goggles: "$discard\n$site=arxiv.org\n$site=*.edu\n$site=huggingface.co"

// Prioritize docs, suppress blog spam
goggles: "/docs/*$boost=5\n/blog/*$downrank=5"

// Boost actual source code on GitHub
goggles: "$boost=5,site=github.com,inurl=/blob/"
```

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: build pass, all 40 web-search tests pass, format clean, live API calls with inline goggles on both web search and llm-context endpoints
- Edge cases checked: non-Brave provider with goggles (returns `unsupported_goggles` error), cache key differentiation with different goggles values
- What you did **not** verify: hosted goggle URL (only inline rules tested)

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes — new optional parameter, no breaking changes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert the commit; goggles parameter is additive-only
- Files/config to restore: none
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `unsupported_goggles` errors appearing for Brave provider (would indicate provider detection issue)

## Risks and Mitigations

Low — additive optional parameter on existing Brave API calls; no behavior change when goggles is not supplied.

target.title: fix(gateway): bind to loopback alongside tailnet/custom IPs for internal calls
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When using `--bind tailnet` or a custom IP, the gateway only listens on that specific address. Internal operations (`sessions_spawn`, CLI probes, subagent RPC) hardcode connections to `wss://127.0.0.1:18789`, which fails because nothing is listening on loopback.
- **Why it matters:** Users running OpenClaw headless/remote with Tailscale cannot use subagents or session spawning — core orchestration features break silently. Multiple users have reported this as a regression affecting real deployments.
- **What changed:** `resolveGatewayListenHosts()` now returns both the specified IP and `127.0.0.1` when the bind host is a specific IP (not `0.0.0.0` which already binds all interfaces). The `canBind()` helper is hoisted for reuse across all branches.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No auth policy changes. No changes to how `0.0.0.0` (LAN mode) or `127.0.0.1` (loopback mode) behave. No client-side RPC target changes.

Fixes #24011
Fixes #30589
Fixes #50607

## Related

- #29871 — alternative approach (client-side forceLoopback), superseded by this fix
- #30618 — related tailnet RPC target fix
- #50751 — related CLI gateway host resolution

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration

## Verification

Ran:

```bash
pnpm test src/gateway/net.test.ts
```

Passed: `6/6` tests covering loopback, LAN, and specific-IP modes with various bindability scenarios.

Live verification:
- Gateway listens on both Tailscale IP and localhost
- `openclaw channels status --probe` succeeds with tailnet binding
- `sessions_spawn` no longer fails with connection refused

## Why this approach over client-side fixes

An alternative approach (#29871) forces every internal `callGateway()` callsite to use loopback. That requires threading `forceLoopback` through multiple files and call paths. This PR fixes the problem at the source — the gateway simply binds to both addresses — which is simpler, less invasive, and requires no client-side changes.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

target.title: Fix media completion duplicate delivery
target.body:
## Summary

- Reuse the active session transcript write lock for transcript appends so media completion wakeups do not self-contend with session-locked writes.
- Make media generation duplicate guards distinguish active work from exact recent-success retries using stable request keys for image, video, and music tools.
- Keep generated-media tasks active through completion delivery, fail them if delivery cannot be confirmed, and use direct generated-media delivery as a fallback for message-tool-only routes.
- Add regression coverage for active/recent duplicate guards, completion delivery ordering, direct media fallback, task registry freshness, and transcript lock reuse.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/video-generation-task-status.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.status.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-tool.test.ts src/agents/tools/image-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/video-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/music-generate-background.test.ts src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `git diff --check`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview`
- `npm pack --silent --pack-destination /tmp/openclaw-local-proof-latest`
- Installed `/tmp/openclaw-local-proof-latest/openclaw-2026.5.17.tgz` globally and restarted the local gateway.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Discord/Rosita image-to-video follow-up no longer starts another still-image generation for the same media completion flow. Completion delivery is confirmed before the media task is marked terminal, and failed delivery/provider runs do not poison the recent-success duplicate guard.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw gateway installed from this branch tarball, Discord server `Rosita`, channel `#general`, session `agent:main:discord:channel:1506010994416025762`, proof marker `PRPROOF-5384`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Installed packed tarball `fa880b2f38f7c8e0812ee14416e53f9f86ce65a619d82875e91839f30fac3167`, restarted gateway, sent one Discord image request for `PRPROOF-5384`, then sent image-to-video follow-up requests in the same Rosita `#general` session.

Evidence after fix: Screenshot and task ledger artifact: https://gist.github.com/fuller-stack-dev/9e2e4f6752f142447bd59ec289dac424

![Rosita #general proof](https://gist.githubusercontent.com/fuller-stack-dev/9e2e4f6752f142447bd59ec289dac424/raw/discord-prproof-5384-rosita-general-cropped.svg)

Observed result after fix: The task ledger shows exactly one `image_generation` succeeded for `PRPROOF-5384`; the actual video tool retry succeeded as `video_generation` and delivered `video-1---61cb9662-4b01-490d-9844-d7531cac8e54.mp4` in Rosita `#general`. A prior xAI video attempt timed out, and the retry was allowed instead of being incorrectly blocked by stale duplicate state.
